children.heading: Budget approval; civil fines; fees; subpoenas; permanent orders
children.label: a Budget approval; civil fines; fees; subpoenas; permanent orders

children.text: upon the recommendation of the Chairman, to approve the annual budget of the Commission as provided in ;section 2717 of this title
to adopt regulations for the assessment and collection of civil fines as provided in ;section 2713(a) of this title
by an affirmative vote of not less than 2 members, to establish the rate of fees as provided in ;section 2717 of this title
by an affirmative vote of not less than 2 members, to authorize the Chairman to issue subpoenas as provided in ; andsection 2715 of this title

by an affirmative vote of not less than 2 members and after a full hearing, to make permanent a temporary order of the Chairman closing a gaming activity as provided in .section 2713(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Monitoring; inspection of premises; investigations; access to records; mail; contracts; hearings; oaths; regulations
children.label: b Monitoring; inspection of premises; investigations; access to records; mail; contracts; hearings; oaths; regulations

children.text: shall monitor class II gaming conducted on Indian lands on a continuing basis;
shall inspect and examine all premises located on Indian lands on which class II gaming is conducted;
shall conduct or cause to be conducted such background investigations as may be necessary;

may demand access to and inspect, examine, photocopy, and audit all papers, books, and records respecting gross revenues of class II gaming conducted on Indian lands and any other matters necessary to carry out the duties of the Commission under this chapter;
may use the United States mail in the same manner and under the same conditions as any department or agency of the United States;
may procure supplies, services, and property by contract in accordance with applicable Federal laws and regulations;

may enter into contracts with Federal, State, tribal and private entities for activities necessary to the discharge of the duties of the Commission and, to the extent feasible, contract the enforcement of the Commission’s regulations with the Indian tribes;
may hold such hearings, sit and act at such times and places, take such testimony, and receive such evidence as the Commission deems appropriate;
may administer oaths or affirmations to witnesses appearing before the Commission; and
shall promulgate such regulations and guidelines as it deems appropriate to implement the provisions of this chapter.

children.heading: Application of Government Performance and Results Act
children.label: d Application of Government Performance and Results Act

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In carrying out any action under this chapter, the Commission shall be subject to the Government Performance and Results Act of 1993 (; ).Public Law 103–62107 Stat. 285

children.heading: Plans
children.label: 2 Plans
children.text: In addition to any plan required under the Government Performance and Results Act of 1993 (; ), the Commission shall submit a plan to provide technical assistance to tribal gaming operations in accordance with that Act.Public Law 103–62107 Stat. 285
